{{Quote|Back on Muunilinst, Obi-Wan leads a team of Lancer Troops, specialized clone troopers mounted on speeder bikes. Durge and his mercenaries prove to be a stout challenge to Obi-Wan and his army. Meanwhile, the ARC troopers penetrate the enemy command center on another part of the planet.|StarWars.com summary|<ref name="SW.com">{{SWArchive|url=clonewars/microseries/chapter/8.html|text=''Clone Wars'': Chapter Eight (small)}}</ref>}}
"'''Chapter 8'''" is the eighth episode of the ''[[Star Wars: Clone Wars]]'' animated television series, and the eighth episode in [[Clone Wars: Season One|Season One]] and [[Star Wars: Clone Wars Volume One|Volume One]]. Directed by [[Genndy Tartakovsky]], the episode originally aired on [[Cartoon Network]] on [[November 18]], [[2003]].

==Plot summary==
On [[Muunilinst/Legends|Muunilinst]], [[Able-472]], one of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi's]] [[Clone trooper/Legends|clone troopers]] in charge of the artillery reports that [[Durge/Legends|Durge]] and his [[Swoop/Legends|swoop]]-mounted [[IG lancer droid/Legends|IG lancer combat droids]] are tearing through their [[Self-Propelled Heavy Artillery]] tanks. Kenobi orders his troops to mount up, and an army of [[Cloning/Legends|clones]] jump onto their [[105-K lancer bike|speeder bikes]] to intercept the droids. The two bands of swoop riders enter into a mass joust, both clone and [[Droid/Legends|droid]] alike being impaled or thrown aside. During the joust, Obi-Wan and Durge both slay many of their respective enemies. During a time in which both sides have great casualties, the two commanders engage in personal combat.

As they fight, Durge deprives Kenobi of his lance, so Kenobi brandishes his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] and destroys [[Durge's swoop bike]] with it. The swoop explodes, but the [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] walks unharmed from the inferno. Kenobi charges in to skewer Durge, but in a display of speed, strength and coordination, the bounty hunter catches Kenobi's swoop by the steering vanes and flips the Jedi off. Durge then charges Kenobi, wielding the swoop as a club, but Kenobi slices it to pieces and stabs Durge through the chest.

Due to his being a [[Gen'Dai/Legends|Gen'Dai]], Durge easily survives the stabbing, and proceeds to laugh and beat Kenobi with his fists. Kenobi, with his saber still lodged in Durge's chest, can't retaliate, and is lifted bodily off the ground by a solid punch in the gut. Durge proceeds to fire at Kenobi with a small rapid [[Gatling gauntlet|projectile launcher]], but the Jedi deflects the slugs with the Force. Switching to a [[ZX miniature flame projector/Legends|flame thrower]], Durge's attacks are again [[Tutaminis|deflected]] and Kenobi crushes the flame thrower with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]]. Brandishing a massive flail, Durge attempts to finish off Kenobi, but Kenobi uses the Force to summon his lightsaber and severs the flails chain. Brandishing his two [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistols]], Durge is disarmed before he can use them. Switching on energy shields in his gauntlets, he deflects Kenobi's lightsaber slashes, but is unable to adequately counter-attack. Kenobi presses his advantage, with Durge barely blocking, until finally, Kenobi slices off Durge's arm and bisects him through the middle. After seemingly defeating Durge, he proceeds to join the ARC troopers. [[Captain/Legends|Captain]] [[Fordo]] reports that his troopers are in position. Taking off on his speeder bike, he leaves Durge's remains, which, unknown to the [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]], begin to rejoin and heal themselves.
